pattern: Sieve the primes, then for each right end slide a window of monotonic deques to bound the prime spread and binary-search how many starts still hold at least two primes.
hints: Only the prime values inside a subarray matter. Precompute which entries are prime, and a prefix count of primes so you can ask how many primes lie in any range.

As a window grows to the left, its prime maximum can only rise and its prime minimum can only fall, so the spread is monotonic; for each right end there is an earliest left where max-min <= k still holds.
Maintain the prime max and min with two monotonic deques to find that earliest left, then count the starts in that range whose subarray contains at least two primes using the prefix counts.

edge_inputs.description: only one prime present yields zero
k of zero needs two equal primes
primes too far apart for small k
repeated single prime gives equal pairs

stress.description: random small values so primes appear frequently and windows vary
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(1500, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 60) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 40)
    return [nums, k]

complexity.time: O(n log n + maxval log log maxval)
